criteria.name: Problem Translation
criteria.description: Ability to translate the ill-defined problem into a clear, well-defined task with specific objectives related to sorting and searching algorithms.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Clearly identifies and articulates the sorting or searching problem, breaking it down into specific tasks such as choosing an appropriate algorithm based on input size and constraints.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to clearly define the problem or selects irrelevant objectives not related to sorting or searching.

criteria.name: Algorithm Selection
criteria.description: Appropriateness of the selected algorithm for the problem context, considering factors like time complexity and input characteristics.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Selects an algorithm that is optimal for the problem constraints, such as using merge sort for large datasets requiring stable sorting.
criteria.poor_indicator: Selects an inappropriate algorithm, such as using bubble sort for large datasets where performance is critical.

criteria.name: Constraint Identification
criteria.description: Identifies constraints and requirements specific to the sorting or searching task, such as stability, memory usage, or time complexity.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately identifies all relevant constraints, including stability requirements and resource limitations.
criteria.poor_indicator: Overlooks key constraints, leading to an incomplete or incorrect problem definition.

typical_errors: Misidentifying the type of sorting required (e.g., stable vs. unstable).
Choosing a sorting algorithm without considering time complexity.
Ignoring specific constraints like memory limitations or input size.

criteria.name: Error Identification
criteria.description: Ability to identify errors or inefficiencies in the AI-generated sorting or searching solution.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Accurately identifies all errors or inefficiencies, such as misordered elements or suboptimal time complexity.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to recognize significant errors or inefficiencies, such as incorrect ordering or unnecessary operations.

criteria.name: Gap Analysis
criteria.description: Skill in identifying missing elements or steps in the AI's solution that are critical for correctness or efficiency.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Identifies all missing steps or elements, such as initialization of variables or handling edge cases.
criteria.poor_indicator: Overlooks missing steps that lead to incorrect or incomplete solutions.

criteria.name: Algorithmic Consistency
criteria.description: Evaluates whether the AI's solution maintains consistency with the chosen algorithm's principles.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Ensures that the AI's solution adheres strictly to the principles and steps of the selected algorithm.
criteria.poor_indicator: Allows deviations from the algorithm's principles that compromise solution integrity.

typical_errors: Failing to detect incorrect element comparisons or swaps.
Missing critical steps in the algorithm execution, like partitioning in quicksort.

criteria.name: Corrective Commands
criteria.description: Effectiveness of the commands given to correct the AI's errors or omissions.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Provides precise and actionable commands that directly address identified errors or omissions, improving the AI's output.
criteria.poor_indicator: Issues vague or ineffective commands that do not resolve the identified issues.

criteria.name: Feedback Integration
criteria.description: Ability to integrate AI feedback into subsequent corrections and improvements.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Seamlessly incorporates AI feedback into iterative improvements, demonstrating adaptive learning.
criteria.poor_indicator: Ignores or misinterprets AI feedback, resulting in repeated errors.

criteria.name: Efficiency Optimization
criteria.description: Commands focus on optimizing the efficiency of the AI's solution in terms of time and space complexity.
criteria.excellent_indicator: Successfully refines the AI's approach to optimize both time and space complexity, such as reducing unnecessary iterations.
criteria.poor_indicator: Fails to address inefficiencies, resulting in suboptimal performance.

typical_errors: Providing commands that do not address the root cause of the error.
Ignoring AI feedback that could improve the next iteration.
Neglecting to optimize for efficiency, leading to unnecessarily complex solutions.
